error.signature: could not import — Go package in directory with hyphen causes import path mismatch
error.regex: could not import|package.*not found.*-|import.*hyphen|invalid import path

verdict.summary: Go package directory named with hyphens (my-package) creates a mismatch because Go package names cannot contain hyphens.

dead_ends.action: Use the directory name as-is in package declaration
dead_ends.why_fails: Go compiler rejects hyphens in package names; package mypackage in dir my-package causes import confusion

dead_ends.action: Use replace directive to map hyphenated path
dead_ends.why_fails: Replace directive remaps modules, not individual packages within a module; does not fix the naming issue

workarounds.action: Rename directory to use underscores or no separator, match package declaration name
workarounds.how: mv my-package mypackage  # directory name should match or be importable as package name

workarounds.action: If external module, import by module path and alias the package name
workarounds.how: import mypackage "github.com/org/my-package/subpkg"  // alias resolves the hyphen
